# Medicine — Skill Arm (live)

**Date:** 2026-06-28 · **Faculty:** Medizinische Fakultät · **Arm:** SKILL (followed `find-university-chairs/SKILL.md` end-to-end)

## Persona (6 dimensions)

- **Interests:** Neurodegenerative diseases (Parkinson's, Alzheimer's, dementias, ataxias); clinical brain research
- **Methods:** Translational/clinical research, molecular & cellular neuropathology, genetics/genomics, biomarker cohort studies
- **Domain:** Neurology / clinical neuroscience / healthcare
- **Thesis style:** Empirical/experimental (wet-lab + clinical data analysis), mixed
- **Skills:** Molecular biology lab methods, histology, some R/Python for data analysis
- **No-gos:** Pure theory/proofs; pure software-engineering theses; no heavy patient-facing clinical rotation as the *primary* activity

## Route (search-strategy §2)

Primary: Medicine → institutes + Kliniken. Interfaculty leg: Hertie Institute for Clinical Brain Research (HIH), DZNE Tübingen, Tübingen Neuro Campus.

## Pass 1 — backbone crawl

- `medizin.uni-tuebingen.de/.../institute` → brain-relevant institutes: **Hertie-Institut für klinische Hirnforschung (HIH)**, Hertie Institute for AI in Brain Health, Neuroanatomie, Neurophysiologie, Neuropathologie, Neuromodulation & Neurotechnologie.
- HIH research page → 7 departments: Neurology w/ Neurovascular Medicine (Ziemann), **Neurodegenerative Diseases (Gasser)**, Neurology & Epileptology (Lerche), Neuro-Oncology (Tabatabai), Neural Dynamics & MEG (Siegel), **Cellular Neurology (Jucker)**, N3 Neurorehab/Neuroprosthetics (Giese).
- DZNE Tübingen → 6 research groups + affiliated HIH/CIN teams.

## Pass 2 — enrichment (relevance + recency)

## Option Map (grouped by interest dimension)

### Neurodegeneration — clinical genetics & translational

**1. Dept. of Neurodegenerative Diseases — Prof. Dr. Thomas Gasser** ([hih-tuebingen.de](https://www.hih-tuebingen.de/en/research/))
- Relevance: Genetics of Parkinson's disease; core neurodegeneration department, also DZNE Tübingen group. Direct hit on interests + genetics method.
- Pros/difficulties: Flagship group, strong DZNE funding; likely very competitive, supervision may be via senior postdocs.
- Evidence: HIH research page + DZNE Tübingen group list, 2024/2025 activity.
- Conversation starter: Ask about a Masterarbeit on monogenic Parkinson's variant characterization in patient cohorts.

**2. Section Translational Genomics of Neurodegenerative Diseases — Prof. Dr. Matthis Synofzik** ([hih-tuebingen.de/synofzik-lab](https://www.hih-tuebingen.de/en/research/independent-research-groups/synofzik-lab))
- Relevance: Ataxias, RNA therapy, clinical neurogenetics — matches translational/genomics method + clinical brain research.
- Pros/difficulties: Very active (RNA-therapy pioneer), strong clinical-research pipeline; topic specificity may narrow thesis options.
- Evidence: Independent research groups page, 2024/2025.
- Conversation starter: Master's on genotype–phenotype analysis in hereditary ataxia registries.

**3. Section/Group Clinical Neurogenetics — Prof. Dr. Ludger Schöls** ([dzne.de Tübingen](https://www.dzne.de/en/research/research-areas/clinical-research/tuebingen/))
- Relevance: Clinical neurogenetics of neurodegenerative disease, DZNE Tübingen.
- Pros/difficulties: Clinical + lab bridge; clinical embedding could pull toward patient work (partial no-go — flagged).
- ⚠ possible conflict with no-go: clinical patient involvement — confirm thesis is lab/data-based.
- Conversation starter: Biomarker analysis in spastic paraplegia / ataxia cohorts.

### Neurodegeneration — cellular & molecular mechanisms

**4. Dept. of Cellular Neurology — Prof. Dr. Mathias Jucker** ([hih-tuebingen.de/jucker-lab](https://www.hih-tuebingen.de/en/research/cellular-neurology/research-groups-and-units/jucker-lab/))
- Relevance: Cellular/molecular mechanisms of brain aging & Alzheimer's, cerebral amyloidoses, neurofilament-light biomarkers. Strong fit for wet-lab + molecular methods.
- Pros/difficulties: High-profile (NfL trajectory work in autosomal-dominant AD, 2024; organoid/microglia co-cultures, 2025); competitive, animal-model work may be involved.
- Evidence: Jucker lab page; 2024/2025 publications.
- Conversation starter: Master's on amyloid seeding or NfL as a progression biomarker.

**5. Group Genome Biology of Neurodegenerative Diseases — Prof. Dr. Peter Heutink** ([dzne.de Tübingen](https://www.dzne.de/en/research/research-areas/clinical-research/tuebingen/))
- Relevance: Genome biology / functional genomics of neurodegeneration (DZNE Tübingen).
- Pros/difficulties: Computational + wet-lab genomics; check current group status (DZNE group composition shifts).
- Conversation starter: Single-cell/genomics analysis of neurodegeneration risk loci.

**6. Group Mitochondria & Inflammation in Neurodegenerative Diseases — Jun.-Prof. Dr. Michela Deleidi** ([dzne.de Tübingen](https://www.dzne.de/en/research/research-areas/clinical-research/tuebingen/))
- Relevance: Mitochondrial dysfunction & neuroinflammation in Parkinson's; iPSC models. Matches cellular/molecular methods.
- Pros/difficulties: Junior group (good supervision access) but may have moved — verify current Tübingen affiliation.
- Conversation starter: iPSC-derived neuron model of mitochondrial dysfunction in PD.

### Adjacent (surfaced, lower priority for this persona)

- Dept. Neurology & Epileptology — Prof. Holger Lerche (channelopathies; partial overlap, not core neurodegeneration).
- Dept. Neural Dynamics & MEG — Prof. Markus Siegel (systems neuroscience, not neurodegeneration).
- Dept. Neuro-Oncology — Prof. Ghazaleh Tabatabai (oncology, not neurodegeneration — excluded as off-topic).

> **Coverage caveat:** This map covers publicly visible chairs/groups as of 2026-06-28. Groups with a weak web presence may be missing. To catch them: visit the HIH/DZNE listing pages directly, ask the Fachschaft Medizin, and check the official Vorlesungsverzeichnis / TübingenNeuroCampus lab list.
